judgment.summary: JNJ maintains strong margins and cash flow despite significant recent declines in earnings growth.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last reported EPS 2.70 vs estimate 2.68 (beat); next earnings date 2026-07-15.
positives: Forward P/E 18.01 vs trailing 26.50 — suggests a more attractive valuation on a forward basis.
Profit margin 21.8% and ROE 26.4% — demonstrate strong profitability and efficient use of equity.
Free cash flow (TTM) $12.51B — provides a substantial cash cushion.
Beta 0.26 — indicates very low price volatility.
negatives: Earnings growth -52.9% — shows a massive contraction in annual profitability.
Quick ratio 0.69 — indicates potential difficulty meeting immediate short-term obligations.
PEG ratio 2.95 — suggests the valuation is high relative to expected growth.
Quarterly earnings growth -52.4% — reflects a sharp recent decline in earnings.

news.paragraph: Johnson & Johnson is currently highlighted for its dividend strength and potential as a value play within the Dow Jones Industrial Average. Recent lung cancer data is also impacting the company's oncology valuation, while a Supreme Court decision regarding Medicare pricing provides a favorable regulatory development for the broader pharmaceutical sector.
